name: Los Santos de la Humosa — circular del Pueblo y Los Miradores
region_intro: Hiking around Los Santos De La Humosa offers diverse landscapes within the Henares River basin. The region features riverine plains, cultivated fields, and elevated plateaus, providing varied terrain. Dramatic geological elements include the cliffs of the Henares plateau, while the "páramo" offers extensive views. From various points, hikers can see the Sierra de Guadarrama and the Sierra del Ocejón. Best hiking trails around Los Santos De La Humosa The most popular hiking route is Alcalá de Henares to Los Santos de la Humosa Loop, a difficult 11.2 miles (18.0 km) trail that takes 5 hours to complete. This route follows the Río Henares and ascends to Los Santos de la Humosa, featuring significant elevation changes. Another top favourite among local hikers is Los Santos de la Humosa — circular del Pueblo y Los Miradores, an easy 4.1 miles (6.6 km) path. This circular route explores the town and its viewpoints. Local hikers also love the Azuqueca de Henares, a 2.7 miles (4.4 km) trail leading through riverine plains, often completed in about 1 hour 8 minutes. Hiking around Los Santos De La Humosa is defined by the Henares River valley, elevated plateaus, and panoramic viewpoints. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy walks to more challenging ascents. The routes in Los Santos De La Humosa are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.6 stars from over 190 reviews. More than 1600 hikers have used komoot to explore Los Santos De La Humosa's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: What types of landscapes can I expect to see while hiking around Los Santos De La Humosa?
A: The region offers a diverse range of landscapes, including riverine plains along the Henares River, cultivated fields, low mountains, and elevated plateaus. You'll also encounter dramatic geological features like the cliffs of the Henares plateau and extensive views from the high "páramo" areas. Distant vistas of the Sierra de Guadarrama and Sierra del Ocejón are visible from various points.

Q: Are there hiking trails suitable for beginners or those looking for an easy walk?
A: Yes, there are many easy options. Out of 157 total hiking routes, 77 are classified as easy. A good example is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1342769308/los-santos-de-la-humosa-circular-del-pueblo-y-los-miradores" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Los Santos de la Humosa — circular del Pueblo y Los Miradores&lt;/a&gt;, an easy 6.6 km circular route that explores the town and its scenic viewpoints.

Q: What are some notable viewpoints or natural features to look out for on the trails?
A: The area is known for its panoramic viewpoints. You can find breathtaking views of the Henares Valley from spots like Mirador del Pico Peñabermeja and Mirador de Los Santos de la Humosa. Other notable overlooks include the balconies of Castillejo Bajo and La Visera. The Henares River itself and its riverbanks offer picturesque natural beauty.

Q: Are there any historical landmarks or attractions near the hiking routes?
A: Yes, the historic 16th-17th century Church of San Pedro Apóstol, perched atop the town, is a prominent landmark offering spectacular views of the Henares plain and valley. Additionally, you might encounter natural monuments such as &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3031028" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Fuente Grande de Corpa&lt;/a&gt; or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6444573" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;White Poplar of Local Interest&lt;/a&gt; along the Henares River.

Q: What is the general difficulty level of hikes in Los Santos De La Humosa?
A: The trails cater to a range of abilities. While there are 77 easy routes and 76 moderate routes, there are also 4 more challenging options for experienced hikers. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1342799690/alcala-de-henares-to-los-santos-de-la-humosa-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Alcalá de Henares to Los Santos de la Humosa Loop&lt;/a&gt; is a difficult 18 km trail with significant elevation changes.

Q: Can I find circular hiking routes in the area?
A: Yes, circular routes are available. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1342769308/los-santos-de-la-humosa-circular-del-pueblo-y-los-miradores" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Los Santos de la Humosa — circular del Pueblo y Los Miradores&lt;/a&gt; is a popular easy circular trail. Many other routes are designed as loops, allowing you to start and end in the same location.

Q: What do other hikers say about the trails in Los Santos De La Humosa?
A: The routes in Los Santos De La Humosa are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.6 stars from over 190 reviews. Hikers often praise the varied terrain, the extensive views, and the opportunity to explore both riverine areas and elevated plateaus.

Q: Are there any specific peaks or summits I can hike to?
A: Yes, the region features several peaks and summits that offer elevated perspectives. You can explore areas like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2391975" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Malvecino Summit&lt;/a&gt;, &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4618274" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ecce-Homo summit&lt;/a&gt;, or &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2473200" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Alto de los Reventones&lt;/a&gt; for higher vantage points.

Q: What is the typical duration of hikes in this area?
A: Hike durations vary significantly depending on the route's length and difficulty. For example, an easy route like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1342831681/azuqueca-de-henares" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Azuqueca de Henares&lt;/a&gt; can be completed in just over an hour, while more challenging trails such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1342799690/alcala-de-henares-to-los-santos-de-la-humosa-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Alcalá de Henares to Los Santos de la Humosa Loop&lt;/a&gt; can take around 5 hours.

Q: Are there any forest parks or natural areas to explore?
A: Yes, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6115319" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Los Cerros Forest Park&lt;/a&gt; is a notable natural area where you can enjoy wooded trails and a different kind of scenery compared to the open plains and plateaus.

Q: Is Los Santos De La Humosa considered a good destination for outdoor activities?
A: Absolutely. Its strategic location, often called the 'Balcón del Henares' (Balcony of the Henares), highlights its elevated position and the extensive views it provides over the Henares Valley. This makes it an appealing destination for those seeking diverse outdoor adventures and scenic hikes.
tips: Nice viewpoint, next to the park there is a fountain
Located on a hill overlooking the town of Los Santos de la Humosa, the Church of San Pedro rises above the Henares plain and the valley of the Henares River.
Small round bullring in the town of Los Santos de la Humosa. This ancient tradition is still valid in this part of Spain.
Return from the viewpoint going back along the same track. Very dangerous to go down another place.
